source.title: Pharmacogenetics of modafinil after sleep loss: catechol-O-methyltransferase genotype modulates waking functions but not recovery sleep
source.finding: COMT Val158Met strongly predicts modafinil efficacy. Val/Val (GG) subjects maintained baseline cognitive performance on 200mg modafinil during 40h sleep deprivation; Met/Met (AA) subjects showed virtually no benefit from the same dose.

AA.phenotype: Low COMT Activity (Met/Met) — Poor Modafinil Responder
AA.effect: High baseline prefrontal dopamine. Modafinil was virtually ineffective at maintaining cognitive performance and vigilant attention during sleep deprivation. Already-saturated dopamine system leaves little room for modafinil's dopaminergic action.
AA.recommendation: Modafinil may have minimal benefit. Consider alternative wakefulness agents or higher doses with close monitoring. Armodafinil may offer slightly different pharmacokinetics.

AG.phenotype: Intermediate COMT Activity (Val/Met) — Partial Modafinil Responder
AG.effect: Intermediate dopamine metabolism. Moderate response to modafinil expected.
AG.recommendation: Standard starting dose (100-200mg). Titrate based on response.

GG.phenotype: High COMT Activity (Val/Val) — Good Modafinil Responder
GG.effect: Fast dopamine clearance in prefrontal cortex. Modafinil potently improved vigor, well-being, executive functioning, and sustained vigilant attention throughout 40h sleep deprivation.
GG.recommendation: Standard dose (100-200mg) expected to be effective. Good candidate for modafinil therapy.
